errors.lookDirectionType: look() expects a string direction: "left", "right", or "ahead"

describers.look: Look in a direction and see what's there

checks.notReachedEnd: You didn't reach the end of the maze.
checks.extraTurn: You seem to have done an extra unnecessary turn at the end.
checks.lookOnlyOnce: look() should only appear once in your whole program.
checks.maxExtraLines: You should add no more than 13 lines of code.

straightPath.name: A straight path
straightPath.description: Implement canMove() so the character can move forward to the end of the maze.

turnLeft.name: Turn left if you can
turnLeft.description: Implement canTurnLeft() so the character turns left when there's a path.

turnRight.name: Turn right if you can't move straight or left
turnRight.description: Implement canTurnRight() so the character turns right when it can't go left or straight.

turnAround.name: Turn around if needed
turnAround.description: Handle dead ends by turning around. All three sensing functions should now work together to solve any maze.

bonusChallenges.name: Bonus challenges
bonusChallenges.description: Can you only use the look() function once in the whole program? And can you solve it by only adding 13 lines of code?

maze1.name: Guide person to the end of the maze

forks.name: Choose left if you can, otherwise choose right
forks.description: A maze with forks that tests left-priority

turnAround.name: Turn around at a dead end
turnAround.description: A maze that requires turning around

forks2.name: Complex maze with forks and dead ends
forks2.description: A complex maze testing the full algorithm

coverOldGround.name: Cover old ground if you need to
coverOldGround.description: A maze where you must backtrack to find the exit

bonus1.name: Only write look() once
bonus1.description: Solve the maze with look() appearing only once in your code

bonus2.name: Add only 13 lines
bonus2.description: Solve the maze by adding only 13 lines of code

start.question: Where do I even start when the program won't run at all?
start.answer: Define `canTurnLeft()`, `canTurnRight()`, and `canMove()` at the top of your code, each just returning `false` for now. The program will then run and you can replace the bodies one at a time.

findDirection.question: How do I find out what is in a given direction?
findDirection.answer: Use the new `look(direction)` function. Pass `"left"`, `"right"`, or `"ahead"` and it returns a string like `"empty"`, `"wall"`, `"fire"`, or `"poop"`.

safeResults.question: Which results mean it is safe to go that way?
safeResults.answer: Of the six things `look()` can return, three are things you really don't want to walk into. Your function should return `true` only when what you see is none of those three.

avoidRepetition.question: I am repeating the same logic in three functions. How do I avoid that?
avoidRepetition.answer: The three functions differ only in which direction they look. Could you write one extra function that takes a direction as an input, and have all three use it?

move.description: Moves the character **one step forward** in the current direction. Walking into a wall, fire, or poop ends your program with an error, so check what's ahead before moving!

turnLeft.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the left** (counterclockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

turnRight.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the right** (clockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

look.description: Looks in the given direction and returns what's there. The direction must be `"left"`, `"right"`, or `"ahead"`. Returns one of: `"empty"`, `"wall"`, `"fire"`, `"poop"`, `"start"`, or `"target"`.
